mcqs.question: Statistical inference is the process of:

options.text: Summarizing data using charts and graphs
Drawing conclusions about a population based on sample data
Collecting data from the entire population
Organizing raw data into frequency tables

mcqs.explanation: Statistical inference involves using sample data to make generalizations or draw conclusions about an unknown population. Options A and D describe descriptive statistics, while option C describes a census, not inference.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a population parameter?

options.text: Sample mean (x-bar)
Sample variance (s^2)
Population mean (mu)
Sample proportion (p-hat)

mcqs.explanation: The population mean (mu) is a parameter because it describes a characteristic of the entire population. Options A, B, and D are statistics because they are computed from sample data.

mcqs.question: A statistic is called unbiased if:

options.text: It always equals the population parameter exactly
Its expected value equals the population parameter it estimates
It is computed from a very large sample
The sample is selected using stratified sampling

mcqs.explanation: An unbiased estimator is one whose expected value (average over all possible samples) equals the true population parameter. It does not mean every individual estimate equals the parameter, only that there is no systematic over- or under-estimation.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of statistical inference?

options.text: Point estimation
Interval estimation
Hypothesis testing
Frequency distribution

mcqs.explanation: Frequency distribution is a tool of descriptive statistics used to organize and summarize data. The two main types of statistical inference are estimation (point and interval) and hypothesis testing.

mcqs.question: In hypothesis testing, the null hypothesis (H0) represents:

options.text: The claim that the researcher wants to prove
The default assumption or status quo about the population parameter
The hypothesis that is always accepted
A hypothesis that uses sample statistics only

mcqs.explanation: The null hypothesis (H0) is the default assumption or existing claim about a population parameter. It represents the status quo and is assumed true until sufficient sample evidence is found to reject it in favor of the alternative hypothesis (H1).
